steps.title: Identify the key signal
steps.content: The decisive clue is that Davis “had to listen repeatedly to the vinyl recordings, which weren't very clear” to transcribe the lyrics. This necessity to transcribe from unclear sources is what matters.

steps.title: Apply the signal to the choices
steps.content: Davis's need to transcribe from unclear recordings directly implies that reliable transcriptions of the improvised lyrics didn't already exist—if they had been available, she wouldn't have needed such labor-intensive work. The other three describe unsupported claims about popularity trends (A), recording quantities (B), or comparative innovation (D). That matches choice C — C is correct.
